reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): PASS - Function is actively called from request_manager.nim:345 and :380 during peer custody intersection checks. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): PASS - custody_group_count originates from untrusted P2P peer metadata (via lookupCgcFromPeer), which is explicitly in-scope per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json trust_assumptions. Gate 3 (Code Verification): PASS - Verified actual code at peerdas_helpers.nim:50-65. The while loop condition `custody_groups.lenu64 < custody_group_count` combined with `custody_group = ... mod cfg.NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS` (line 58-59) causes the HashSet to saturate at 128 unique elements. When peer-advertised custody_group_count exceeds 128, the loop condition can never be satisfied, causing an infinite loop. Gate 4 (Exploitability): PASS - An attacker can trigger this via P2P by advertising custody_group_count = 2^32 in peer metadata. When victim node calls matchIntersection or checkPeerCustody during data column sync, resolve_columns_from_custody_groups invokes handle_custody_groups with the malicious value, freezing the calling thread. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): PASS - Property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-047 from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_MANUAL_15_109_190_216.json explicitly requires: 'handle_custody_groups must terminate in O(NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS) iterations regardless of the peer-advertised value.' The code violates this assertion by allowing unbounded loop iterations. Gate 6 (Scope Check): PASS - This is a P2P-triggered DoS affecting PeerDAS (Fusaka-specific EIP-7594), requires only a single malicious metadata packet (not high-effort), and targets in-scope P2P network components. Severity Calibration: Original severity is Medium. Impact: Single-peer DoS freezing sync thread when processing peer custody metadata. Does not affect entire network but degrades node availability. Maintains Medium severity per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json criteria (affects >5% of network if multiple peers exploit simultaneously, though single-node impact is thread-level freeze). An attacker can trigger this via P2P by advertising custody_group_count=2^32 in peer metadata, causing the victim node's sync thread to freeze indefinitely when calling matchIntersection or checkPeerCustody during data column sync operations.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Peer-reported custody_group_count must be validated against NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS upper bound before use in any loop or data structure sizing. handle_custody_groups must terminate in O(NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS) iterations regardless of the peer-advertised value. (Property from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_MANUAL_15_109_190_216.json)
